div.p: Visual examples of unsafe code patterns that fail verification
0: (b7) r0 = 0 1: (b7) r1 = 0 2: (07) r1 += 1 3: (15) if r1 == 1000 goto pc+0 4: (05) goto pc-3 infinite loop detected
4: (61) r1 = *(u32 *)(r10 -4) 5: (67) r1 &= 255 6: (67) r1 <<= 2 7: (0f) r0 += r1 invalid access to map value, value_size=40 off=1020 size=4 R0 max value is outside of the allowed memory range
2: (bf) r1 = r0 3: (77) r1 >>= 32 4: (b7) r2 = 0 5: (15) if r1 == 0 goto pc+1 6: (b7) r2 = 42 7: (61) r0 = *(u32 *)(r10 -4) read from uninitialized stack at offset -4

div.h2: The Verifier Proves Safety for ALL Possible Inputs
div.p: Not just your test cases. If there exists ANY input that could cause:

ul.li: Out-of-bounds access The program is REJECTED.

div.p: Tracks possible values (min/max/type) for every register at every instruction
Explores ALL branches Merges states at join points Conservative: assumes worst case
Must prove loop terminates Limited instruction count (1M) Bounded complexity
